title: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse

evidence.note: Published 1921; the 95-year US copyright term has expired for works published through 1930, so the film print is public domain in the US by term expiry.

watch.label: Internet Archive — The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse (1921), full film

editorial: The Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse is a 1921 American silent epic war drama directed by Rex Ingram for Metro Pictures, adapted by June Mathis from Vicente Blasco Ibanez's novel. The top-grossing film of 1921, it made Rudolph Valentino a star and stands among the era's most influential early anti-war films. Published in 1921, the film print's 95-year US copyright term has expired, placing the photoplay in the public domain by term expiry.
